summary: Deliver the next M1 C8 slice: current-snapshot OpenSCAD errors and warnings become Appendix C-themed CodeMirror squiggles and gutter markers; structured diagnostics with valid locations become native buttons that activate a matching current or already-open document, move the cursor to the exact 1-based line, scroll it into view, and focus the editor. Invalid, out-of-range, echo, trace, info, stale, and unavailable-file locations remain non-marking or non-interactive. Q-0020 parks only loading a reported path that C6 has not made available.
specSections: Section 2.7 questions, decisions, and unit of work
Appendix C editor and console theme tokens

externalInputs.purpose: Resolve and pin the approved official CodeMirror lint extension at the already-resolved transitive version 6.9.7 as a direct runtime dependency.

decisions.decision: Use the official @codemirror/lint 6.9.7 extension and translate only current error and warning diagnostics with valid in-range 1-based lines into full-line lint ranges; install the lint gutter and always replace its diagnostic set, including with an empty set.
decisions.rationale: The maintained CodeMirror extension provides editor-native ranges, markers, and tooltip semantics, while filtering echo, trace, info, missing, non-integer, non-positive, and out-of-range locations prevents unrelated lines from being marked.

decisions.decision: Resolve a diagnostic target from its project-relative file or the render entry-file fallback, require an exact matching current or open document and a valid source line, route activation through the existing command bus, then issue an ephemeral editor navigation request.
decisions.rationale: This delivers same-file and open-cross-file AC-8.b behavior without manufacturing source bytes, document identities, or filesystem authority for an unloaded path that belongs to C6 and is now isolated as Q-0020.

decisions.decision: Identify each click with a monotonic request id, apply it only to the matching active document, select the reported line start, center-scroll and focus the editor, then acknowledge and clear the request after handling.
decisions.rationale: A request id lets repeated clicks on the same diagnostic work, while clearing after acknowledgement prevents a later manual tab switch from replaying an old navigation through a restored CodeMirror session.

decisions.decision: Override CodeMirror lint's embedded range, point, tooltip-border, active-range, and gutter-marker colors with editor.squiggleError, editor.squiggleWarning, and editor.activeLine tokens; replace embedded SVG marker colors with a token-colored error circle and warning diamond.
decisions.rationale: Installing the official extension must not bypass Appendix C or introduce fixed third-party red/orange visuals into a live-switchable Light, Dark, or High Contrast editor.

decisions.decision: Feed inline diagnostics from the globally current render source snapshot, independent of which participating open document is active, and feed none when any participating source no longer matches; do not claim this conservative clear-on-stale behavior as the final Q-0016 presentation policy.
decisions.rationale: A cross-file diagnostic remains visible after its tab activates when the render snapshot is still current, but no obsolete line marker is installed after any source edit; a future stale presentation can be layered separately if the owner chooses it.

decisions.decision: Treat the open-cross-file test as the UI contract for an already-resolved project-relative diagnostic only; do not claim native include/use-file navigation end-to-end until the subsequent FR-4.5 staging slice writes all project files and maps every engine workspace path back to its logical source.
decisions.rationale: The current native bridge sends only entry source and resolves only main.scad, so injecting a structured parts/wheel.scad diagnostic proves Workbench behavior but not yet the native production path that produces it.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-1.6, FR-8.6, and AC-8.b inline markers and click navigation
testEvidence.test: Errors and warnings create exact-line lint ranges and gutter markers while echo and invalid locations do not; navigation moves and focuses the editor without editing; and a diagnostic button activates an open cross-file target at its reported line with the marker retained from the current render snapshot.

red.outcome: RED: 3 assertion-level tests failed for the intended missing behavior: zero lint ranges/markers, selection remained on line 1 instead of line 2, and no location-bearing diagnostic button existed.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 34 files and 334 tests passed, including exact marker counts, clear-on-empty and controlled-value behavior, direct navigation, stale suppression, open cross-file activation, cursor status, focus, and retained current-snapshot marker.

testEvidence.test: The mounted CodeMirror stylesheet removes the extension's embedded lint background images and contains token-driven error/warning range and gutter-marker rules.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/editor/CodeEditor.theme.test.tsx -t "replaces CodeMirror lint colors" --reporter=verbose
red.outcome: RED: the focused assertion found CodeMirror's embedded fixed lint SVG/color rules and no ScadMill background-image or token-colored marker overrides.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/editor/CodeEditor.theme.test.tsx -t "replaces CodeMirror lint colors" --reporter=verbose
green.outcome: GREEN: the focused token-ownership assertion passed after installing ScadMill's themed range, point, active-range, and gutter overrides.

questions: Q-0016 remains open: this slice conservatively suppresses all inline markers when the source snapshot is stale but does not claim that as the final owner-selected stale-diagnostic UX.
Q-0020 was appended under Section 2.7: active and already-open file navigation proceeds, while loading an unavailable reported path remains parked until the C6 source authority is decided.
Native include/use-file diagnostic production is not claimed by this UI slice; it remains required in the subsequent M1 FR-4.5 staging and path-mapping work recorded by D-0072.

The browser-hosted shell was verified with one mounted lint gutter, token-driven marker rules, and no browser warnings or errors; native error/warning visuals are covered by the focused CodeMirror and Workbench integration tests because the web shell truthfully remains engine-unavailable before M3.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
